steps.title: Focus on wireless power
steps.content: The passage claims that wireless communication devices can be powered piezoelectrically when resonance supplies steady energy. A supporting finding should show that near-constant kinetic vibration can power such devices without an outside electrical source.

steps.title: Set aside weaker support
steps.content: Choice B explains why intermittent supply is bad, but it does not show piezoelectric powering works for wireless devices. Choice C contradicts compatibility. Choice D shifts to CFRP conductivity rather than the device claim. The remaining choice directly supports piezoelectric wireless-device power. The answer is A.
